🇲🇽 contract-mx.md — Mexican Client
🇺🇸🇨🇦 contract-us-ca.md — US/CA Client
✍️ milestone-signoff.md — Sign-Off
⚖️ MX vs US/CA Key Differences
Filedocs/templates/contracts/contract-mx.md
LawCódigo Civil Federal + Código Civil CDMX
CurrencyMXN + IVA 16%
SignatureNOM-151-SCFI (Mifiel)
DisputeCANACO / CAM arbitration → CDMX courts
1. Objeto del Contrato 2. Honorarios y Pago 3. IVA + CFDI 4.0 4. Plazo y Programa 5. Derechos de Autor (LFDA) 6. Caso Fortuito (CCF 2111) 7. Limitación de Responsabilidad 8. Suspensión y Rescisión 9. Confidencialidad 10. Obligaciones del Cliente 11. Firma Electrónica 12. Legislación y Arbitraje
Loading…
Filedocs/templates/contracts/contract-us-ca.md
LawMexican law (elected) — common law framing
CurrencyUSD (or CAD) — SWIFT wire
SignatureDocuSign / Adobe Sign (ESIGN Act / PIPEDA)
DisputeICC or AAA International arbitration — NY Convention
Art 1. Scope of Services Art 2. Compensation + SWIFT Art 3. Schedule Art 4. IP / Instruments of Service Art 5. Limitation of Liability Art 6. Force Majeure Art 7. Suspension + Termination Art 8. Confidentiality Art 9. Client Obligations Art 10. Governing Law + ICC Arbitration Art 11. Insurance Art 12. General Provisions
Loading…
Filedocs/templates/contracts/milestone-signoff.md
LanguageBilingual ES / EN (parallel)
Used atM1 (contract signing) · M2 (conceptual) · M3 (schematic) · M4 (executive plans)
EffectChange freeze — post-signature modifications require paid change order
Part A — Deliverables Table Part B — Design Decisions Confirmed Part C — Client Declaration Part D — Registered Comments Part E — Payment Authorization Appendix — Change Order Policy
Loading…

MX vs US/CA — Key Differences

Currency & Payment

Issue🇲🇽 Mexican Client🇺🇸🇨🇦 US/CA Client
CurrencyMXNUSD (or CAD)
Tax+16% IVA; CFDI 4.0; SAT code 8591010% IVA if qualifies as export of services (Ley del IVA Art. 29)
WithholdingRetenciones apply if client is persona moralClient grosses up if their jurisdiction requires withholding
Payment methodSPEI / transfer to CLABESWIFT international wire transfer
Payment window5 business days per invoice10 business days per invoice
Late interest2% per month1.5% per month (18% per annum)

Intellectual Property

Issue🇲🇽 Mexican Client🇺🇸🇨🇦 US/CA Client
Legal basisLFDA Arts. 13, 21, 92AIA B101-2017 / Copyright Act / 17 U.S.C.
Moral rightsInalienable — cannot be waived by any clauseNo equivalent in US; Canada allows moral rights waiver by contract
Client getsLicencia de uso — project-specific, non-transferableLimited license — conditional on substantial payment
Termination for convenienceWork in progress non-refundable; license suspended until paidLicensing fee owed if client continues project with another architect
Owner modificationsLFDA Art. 92 — client may modify built work without consentNo statutory rule; license defines limits

Dispute Resolution

Step🇲🇽 Mexican Client🇺🇸🇨🇦 US/CA Client
1. Negotiation15 days direct negotiation30 days direct negotiation
2. MediationCANACO CDMX or CAMICDR/AAA or CAM
3. ArbitrationCANACO or CAM rules; CDMX seat; SpanishICC or AAA International rules; CDMX seat; English; NY Convention
Governing lawCódigo Civil CDMX + CCFMexican law (elected) — protects LFDA moral rights

Signatures

Issue🇲🇽 Mexican Client🇺🇸🇨🇦 US/CA Client
StandardNOM-151-SCFI-2016 (Mifiel, Firel/SAT)ESIGN Act (US) / PIPEDA (Canada) — DocuSign, Adobe Sign
Date stampCertified PSC timestamp required for NOM-151Platform timestamp sufficient

Force Majeure

Issue🇲🇽 Mexican Client🇺🇸🇨🇦 US/CA Client
Statutory backstopCCF Art. 2111 applies even without a clauseNo statutory backstop — clause is essential
Termination trigger60 days of event continuing90 days of event continuing

Termination by Client

Issue🇲🇽 Mexican Client🇺🇸🇨🇦 US/CA Client
Notice7 calendar days written notice7 calendar days written notice
WIP refundNot refundableNot refundable
Licensing feeLicense suspended until paid — no explicit licensing feeLicensing fee owed if client continues project with new architect (AIA B101 §7.3 equivalent)

Key Legal Citations

TopicMX CitationUS/CA Citation
Professional servicesCCF Arts. 2606–2615AIA B101-2017
Force majeureCCF Art. 2111Contractual only (frustration doctrine is narrow)
CopyrightLFDA Arts. 13, 21, 9217 U.S.C. / Copyright Act R.S.C. 1985
Electronic signaturesNOM-151-SCFI-2016ESIGN Act / PIPEDA
Rescission for breachCCF Art. 1949Common law breach of contract
Arbitration enforcementMexican law; CANACO/CAM rulesNew York Convention (US + CA + MX all signatories)

Both contracts elect Mexican governing law. Both contracts route disputes to arbitration before litigation. Both protect the Architect's IP through a conditional license — not a transfer of ownership. The SOW (scope-of-work.json) attaches as Exhibit A / Anexo A in both versions.
